definitions: An acronym for 'Domain Name System Extension' or 'Domain Name System Enhancements', referring to various technologies and protocols designed to improve the security, reliability, and functionality of the Domain Name System.
In some contexts, it may refer to 'Defense Nuclear Security Enterprise', an organization or framework related to nuclear security within defense sectors.
examples: The new DNSE protocols enhance internet security.
Implementing DNSE is crucial for robust domain management.
The DNSE initiative aims to protect critical infrastructure.
